rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is GRANTED in the amount of $364,573.20. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA ANDERSON VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2021 line 3. Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. Defendants brought the same arguments as to the sufficiency of allegations on a demurrer to the first amended complaint. The Court sustained the demurrer with leave to amend as to the causes of action subject to both the demurrer and this motion. Thereafter Defendants chose to answer the first amended complaint notwithstanding the Court's order. Defendants cannot now, 5 years into the litigation, bring their effectively waived arguments as to the sufficiency of the pleadings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES JADALLAH VS. BEN CANNON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT BEN CANNON Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473 is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that CCP 473(b) is applicable to discovery motions taken off calendar. Furthermore, assuming that CCP 473(b) is applicable under the circumstances, this motion should have been accompanied by the missing separate statement that the Court requested in the June 8, 2021 ruling: (1) this motion is not accompanied by a separate statement outlining the good faith efforts to meet and confer and the outstanding issues after the meet and confer process; and (2) contrary to Mr. Hain's declaration no such separate statement was filed at any time between June 8, 2021 and June 15, 2021 ("I addressed the failure to meet and confer in the joint separate statement and filed it."). However, if such joint separate statement was in fact filed (and the Court missed the filing in its review of the Register of Actions), Defendant may submit a filed endorsed copy of that filing to the Court no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 20, 2021. The Court notes that the parties have been expressly advised that their unauthorized e-mail communications regarding this motion would not be reviewed or considered by the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
